problem_addressed: Language agents struggle to efficiently learn from trial-and-error as traditional reinforcement learning requires extensive training samples and expensive model fine-tuning.
core_claim: Reflexion demonstrates that language agents can be effectively reinforced through verbal feedback and reflection rather than weight updates, achieving significant performance improvements across diverse tasks.
key_contributions: Proposes Reflexion framework using verbal reinforcement learning for language agents
Achieves 91% pass@1 accuracy on HumanEval coding benchmark, surpassing previous state-of-the-art GPT-4 (80%)
Provides ablation studies and analysis of different feedback signals and incorporation methods
novelty: Unlike traditional reinforcement learning that updates model weights, Reflexion introduces verbal reinforcement learning where agents reflect on task feedback signals and maintain reflective text in episodic memory to improve future decision-making.
method_summary: Reflexion implements an iterative optimization process where language agents interact with environments, receive feedback, verbally reflect on this feedback, and store reflections in episodic memory to improve subsequent trials.
method_components: Actor model that produces trajectories by interacting with environments
Evaluator model that produces scalar rewards based on task performance
Self-Reflection model that analyzes trajectories and rewards to produce verbal feedback summaries
Episodic memory buffer that stores reflective text for future trials
key_innovation: Using verbal reinforcement learning through self-reflection - agents analyze their own failed attempts and generate linguistic feedback that helps them improve future performance without any weight updates to the underlying model.
assumptions: Language agents can effectively analyze their own trajectories and feedback
Verbal reflection can be distilled into useful hints for future decision-making
Limited memory (3 experiences) is sufficient to capture relevant learning
Feedback signals can be either scalar values or free-form language
theoretical_grounding: The paper frames Reflexion as an iterative optimization process with empirical validation across multiple benchmarks, but does not provide formal proofs or mathematical derivations.
headline_result: Reflexion with gpt-4 on HotPotQA achieved 80% accuracy
improvement_over_baseline: Up to 12 percentage points improvement on HotPotQA
stated_limitations: Traditional reinforcement learning methods require extensive training samples and expensive model fine-tuning, which Reflexion aims to address.
unstated_weaknesses: Reflexion doesn't directly address attention mechanisms.
Computational overhead in maintaining and processing the episodic memory buffer.
Effectiveness of verbal reflection might vary depending on task complexity.
threats_to_validity: Generalization to tasks with complex reasoning or less clear feedback signals.
Potential computational inefficiencies due to unbounded episodic memory buffer.
reproducibility: medium (code availability not mentioned in abstract)
open_questions: How does Reflexion compare to other reinforcement learning methods in terms of sample efficiency?
Can the verbal reflection mechanism be optimized for better performance?
How does the episodic memory buffer scale with the number of trials?
future_work: Not explicitly mentioned in the abstract.
gaps_this_enables: Research on using verbal reflection as a form of reinforcement learning.
Exploring alternative ways to reinforce language agents without weight updates.
relevance_to_topic: Reflexion is tangentially related to efficient attention mechanisms as it proposes an alternative to traditional reinforcement learning methods, but doesn't directly address attention mechanisms.
best_result: Reflexion with gpt-4 on HotPotQA achieved 80% accuracy
limitations_stated: Traditional reinforcement learning methods require extensive training samples and expensive model fine-tuning, which Reflexion aims to address.
title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
